calendar.matters:
Plaintiff Machele Webb Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
R. Scott Erlewine, Esq. rse@phillaw.com; Brian Conlon, Esq. bsc@phillaw.com 415-398-0900 of Phillips Erlewine Given & Carlin LLP for Plaintiff.
Michael Miller, Esq. michaelmiller@lightmiller.com 619-554-8460 of Light & Miller LLP for Plaintiff,
Kevin Harlow, Esq. kevin.harlow@us.dlapiper.com 619-699-3402 of DLA Peper LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Machele Webb's Motion For New Trial.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Machele Webb's motion for a new trial - the papers claim four grounds - is denied.

First, plaintiff argues that the factual findings were not supported by the evidence. To the contrary, the summary judgment order's findings - reviewed again in considering this new trial motion - are supported by the evidence cited. Plaintiff's spin on the facts simply repeats arguments already addressed.

Second, plaintiff asserts for the first time that this is a "mixed-motive" case. However, "[n]ew theories that could have been raised, but were not, is not one of the causes that permits a new trial." (Ins. Co. of State of Pa. v. Am. Safety Indemnity Co. (2019) 32 Cal.App.5th 898, 922-23.)

Third, it is claimed that discretion was abused by considering evidence from two "interested" witnesses. Rare is the case without interested witnesses. This evidence was credible and consistent with the other evidence.

Fourth, plaintiff claims "ambush" by theories in defendants' opening summary judgment papers. However, a key purpose of the 75-day notice period for summary judgment motions is to "provide the responding party with adequate time to conduct discovery that may be necessary to fully respond to the motion." (Lackner v. North (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1188, 1208.)

Defendants' Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
